paper_title: Shotgun Environmental DNA, Pollen, and Macrofossil Analysis of Lateglacial Lake Sediments From Southern Sweden

methods.description: This method involves analyzing plant macrofossils and microfossils from lake sediment samples to compare pollen spectra with plant macrofossil assemblages and DNA results.

steps.description: 16 sub-samples were taken from the two cores at exactly the same levels as the samples for DNA analysis.
Sediment samples were soaked in a sodium pyrophosphate solution overnight to disaggregate the clay minerals.
steps.searchable_summary: disaggregate clay minerals in sediment samples using sodium pyrophosphate

steps.description: Samples were sieved using a 250-µm mesh and remains retained on the sieve were identified using a binocular microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sediment samples and identify remains with a microscope

steps.description: For microfossil analyses, subsample 0.5 cm3 of sediment from each of the 16 samples and prepare using standard treatments (HCl, KOH, HF, acetolysis).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment subsamples for microfossil analysis using chemical treatments

steps.description: Slides were mounted in glycerol, and pollen, spores, and coenobia were counted using a light microscope at 400× magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: count pollen, spores, and coenobia using light microscopy

methods.description: This method involves using shotgun sequencing to analyze environmental DNA from sediment samples to identify plant taxa and compare with micro and macrofossil records.

steps.description: Starting from more than a billion reads, only very few reads (<2,000) could eventually be assigned to the likely local plant taxa.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sequencing reads to assign to plant taxa

steps.description: For mapping, plastid DNA sequences present at NCBI were used in combination with plastid DNA and ribosomal DNA derived from the PhyloNorway data set.
steps.searchable_summary: map sequencing reads using plastid and ribosomal DNA references

steps.description: To avoid misidentifications, 90% of the sequences matching to at least two different reference database entries occurring at least two times in a sample were used.
steps.searchable_summary: validate read assignments with reference database
